summary.brief: Neurologist failed to warn patient about impulse control disorder (ICD) risk associated with dopamine agonist medication for Parkinson's disease.
summary.clinical_synopsis: Patient on ropinirole (dopamine agonist) for Parkinson's disease since 2008. By April 2010, warning about ICD risk became mandatory. Neurologist failed to warn, and patient subsequently developed ICD and psychosis.
summary.legal_synopsis: Court found neurologist breached duty by failing to warn about ICD risk from April 2010 onwards. Patient would have stopped medication earlier if properly warned.

evidence_index.text: Mrs Hazel Kennedy is a retired primary school teacher, now aged 56. She is married to Dr Philip Kennedy, who was a consultant neurologist until his retirement in 2006.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In 2006, when aged 44, Mrs Kennedy developed a tremor in her left upper limb.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Frankel saw Mrs Kennedy privately, but without charge. He diagnosed Parkinson's disease and advised on her treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dopamine agonist medication, which the Claimant took on his advice, caused her psychiatric side effects, including an impulse control disorder (ICD) and eventually psychosis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Defendant denies liability. He maintains that his treatment of the Claimant was reasonable at all times, based upon the information available to him at the time.
evidence_index.speaker: Defendant's position

evidence_index.text: Dr Sawle was very clear that he was not critical of Dr Frankel's advice prior to 2010.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: I find therefore that the Defendant was in breach of duty in failing to give a warning at this time.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Claimant first noticed a tremor in her left upper limb sometime in 2006.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: By August 2007, Mrs Kennedy felt that her symptoms had worsened.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: I find a material breach of duty in relation to the advice given in October 2011.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I consider that the involvement of Dr Kennedy did play its part and perhaps explains, although does not excuse, Dr Frankel's breach.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The Claimant and Dr Kennedy recall worsening problems from the end of 2011.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr Sawle acknowledged that there is a balance to be struck between the control of motor symptoms and unwanted side effects.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: I therefore find that the Defendant should have warned the Claimant of the risk of developing ICD on ropinirole from April 2010.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Claimant had an unpleasant time coming off the drug but went on to make a good recovery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Retired primary school teacher, married to a neurologist

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient has been diagnosed with Parkinson's disease since 2007 and has been taking ropinirole (a dopamine agonist) since February 2008. She is here for a routine follow-up to review her medication and symptom control. Her motor symptoms have been reasonably well controlled on the current medication regimen. Recently, her husband has noticed some changes in her behavior, including increased spending and some impulsive decisions, though the patient herself does not think these are problematic.

focused_exam.neurological: Mild left upper limb resting tremor, improved compared to prior visits. Some bradykinesia. Gait stable.

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Parkinson's disease on dopamine agonist therapy - routine follow-up with possible emerging behavioral changes
initial_differential: Possible impulse control disorder secondary to dopamine agonist
critical_findings: Patient on dopamine agonist (ropinirole) for 2+ years
Behavioral changes noted by spouse (increased spending, impulsive decisions)

requestables.name: Neurology Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with Dr. Frankel regarding Parkinson's disease diagnosis

reveal.result_summary: Diagnosis of Parkinson's disease, advised dopamine agonist medication
reveal.clinical_significance: Initiated treatment plan for Parkinson's disease
reveal.decision_impact: Led to prescription of dopamine agonist

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with tremor in left upper limb

timeline_phases.name: Diagnosis and Initial Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Diagnosis of Parkinson's disease and initiation of treatment

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient diagnosed with Parkinson's disease and prescribed dopamine agonist
decision_points.prompt: What should be discussed with the patient regarding medication risks?

options.description: Warn about risk of impulse control disorder (ICD) with dopamine agonists
options.clinical_reasoning: ICD is a known risk with dopamine agonists and should be disclosed

options.description: Do not discuss specific risks of ICD
options.clinical_reasoning: Defendant did not routinely warn about ICD until 2013

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not warn about ICD
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not routinely warn about ICD until 2013

expected_action_court.description: Should have warned about ICD risk
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Mandatory to warn about ICD by April 2010

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to warn about ICD risk was a breach of duty
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Patient should have been informed about ICD risk

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient developed ICD symptoms while on dopamine agonist
decision_points.prompt: What should be the management plan for ICD symptoms?

options.description: Switch to levodopa to manage ICD symptoms
options.clinical_reasoning: Levodopa likely to abolish ICD symptoms while controlling Parkinson's
Defendant increased dose despite ICD symptoms

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not consider ICD symptoms significant

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Switching would have controlled ICD symptoms

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Switch to levodopa should have been recommended

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed impulse control disorder and psychosis
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Psychiatric side effects, recovery after medication change

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to warn about ICD risks
Breach of duty in medication management advice

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to warn about ICD and inappropriate medication management
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper advice would have prevented psychosis

factual_timeline.event: Patient develops tremor in left upper limb
Failure to warn about ICD risks

allegations.allegation: Failure to warn about ICD risk
Failure to warn about ICD risks
